text: The surgeon begins the operation by dissecting the sleeve gastrectomy, separating it from the omentum and dividing the additions at the suture line. The hiatus is carefully dissected, and a Penrose drain is passed around the retrosophageal window. During the detailed dissection of the hiatus, a previous hernia repair with VLOG suture is identified.

The surgeon dissects the esophagus into the mediastinum, achieving three centimeters of intra-abdominal esophagus. The hiatus is closed using two O-etibon sutures. An intraoperative EGD is performed to check for kinks or structures at the proximal sleeve. The sleeve is transected approximately five centimeters from the gastroesophageal junction, and the antrum is resected.
The surgeon performs a jejuno-jejunostomy, followed by a two-layer gastrojejunostomy, creating an 80-centimeter alimentary limb and a 50-centimeter biliopancreatic limb.
